findings.message: Missing return statement on the success path: get_item() is annotated to return Item, but when the item IS found the function falls through without returning it. FastAPI will serialize None against response_model=ItemRead and raise a validation error (or return null), so every successful lookup is broken.
findings.suggestion: Add `return item` after the `if not item: raise HTTPException(...)` block.

findings.message: Falsy check on an ORM result is the wrong idiom for None-detection: `if not item` conflates 'object not found' with 'object is falsy'. Use an explicit identity check against None so the intent is unambiguous and robust to any future __bool__/__len__ on the model.
findings.suggestion: Replace `if not item:` with `if item is None:`.

findings.message: `from fastapi import HTTPException` is imported inside the function body. This works but defers the import error to request time and is inconsistent with the module-level imports already in this file; move it to the top of the module so import failures surface at startup.
findings.suggestion: Hoist `HTTPException` into the existing top-level `from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends` line.
